text:
Cipher Mining Announces December 2022 Operational Update

Press Release Date: January 10, 2023

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR) reported the following December 2022 results:

Bitcoin Mined: 225 BTC
Bitcoin Sold: 39 BTC
Bitcoin Holdings: 394 BTC
Deployed Fleet: 28,000 miners
Operating Hash Rate: 2.8 EH/s (all-time high)

The company energized 8,326 new miners during December, increasing hash rate by approximately 0.8 EH/s — a 40% monthly increase. Bitcoin production jumped 137% compared to November.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "This rapid progress demonstrates the expertise of our amazing deployment and operations teams" and noted the company purchased 7,200 additional rigs while maintaining cost discipline.

Cipher accelerated buildout at its Odessa facility, increasing daily mining potential from approximately 3.6 BTC to 6.6 BTC — an 84% monthly increase. The company holds over 59,000 total rigs for self-mining, with approximately 31,000 additional fully-paid units en route to Odessa.

Management anticipates expanding self-mining capacity to approximately 6 EH/s during the first quarter of 2023.

Cipher Mining Announces January 2023 Operational Update

Date: February 1, 2023

Key Production Metrics for January 2023:
Bitcoin Mined: 343
Bitcoin Sold: 314
Bitcoin Held: 424
Deployed Mining Rigs: 41,000
Month-End Operating Hash Rate: 4.3 EH/s

Cipher Mining achieved significant growth during January, energizing 13,300 new miners that increased hash rate by approximately 1.4 EH/s — representing a 48% monthly increase. The company's daily Bitcoin production potential grew from approximately 9.3 to 14.0 Bitcoin, marking a 51% expansion in a single month.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "Our teams worked tirelessly to continue rapid hash rate build throughout January...Cipher remains on track to build out ~6 EH/s of self-mining capacity in the first quarter of 2023."

The company now operates over 3.3 EH/s of self-mining capacity at its Odessa facility, with approximately 4.3 EH/s across all sites. January production of 343 Bitcoin represented a 52% increase from the previous month. As part of regular treasury management, Cipher sold approximately 314 Bitcoin while building inventory.

Cipher Mining Announces February 2023 Operational Update

Release Date: March 1, 2023

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 398
BTC Sold: 357
BTC Held: 465
Deployed Mining Rigs: 48,500
Operating Hash Rate: 5.2 EH/s

Cipher Mining achieved a new all-time high hash rate capacity of 5.2 EH/s while producing 398 bitcoins in February 2023. The company energized 7,710 new mining units during the month, representing approximately 0.9 EH/s in additional hash rate capacity.

CEO Tyler Page stated that the company maintained its "steady march to deploy an initial 6 EH/s" with over 4.2 EH/s operational at the Odessa facility. He emphasized the company's ability to "build bitcoin inventory during the month while simultaneously paying operating expenses" through production revenue alone.

The company increased its daily mining potential from approximately 13.0 bitcoins to 15.8 bitcoins — a 21% monthly increase. Bitcoin production rose 16% compared to January. As part of standard treasury management, Cipher sold approximately 357 bitcoins while building reserves to 465 bitcoins held at month-end.

Cipher Mining Announces March 2023 Operational Update

Cipher Mining reported producing 413 bitcoins in March 2023 while achieving a new all-time high hash rate capacity of 5.7 EH/s. The company deployed 53,500 mining rigs and sold 450 BTC during the month, ending with 427 BTC held.

The company energized 5,023 new miners, representing approximately 0.5 EH/s increase in hash rate. CEO Tyler Page stated: "With our best-in-class unit economics, we were able to fund ongoing capex for Odessa from operations in March, without resorting to dilutive equity issuance or costly debt financing."

Cipher expanded its Odessa facility to over 4.7 EH/s of self-mining capacity. The company strategically managed power usage, curtailing mining operations during periods to sell power in the market. Management expects to achieve the initial 6 EH/s goal following April miner shipments.

The operational update indicates Cipher finished March with potential to mine nearly 16 bitcoins daily, assuming a network hash rate of 332 EH/s.

Cipher Mining Announces April 2023 Operational Update

Release Date: May 1, 2023

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR)

Cipher Mining announced reaching its initial corporate buildout objective of 6.0 exahashes per second (EH/s) in April 2023. The company produced approximately 406 Bitcoin during the month while deploying over 59,000 mining rigs.

Key Metrics:
Bitcoin Mined: 406
Bitcoin Sold: 406
Bitcoin Held: 427
Deployed Mining Rigs: 59,000
Operating Hash Rate: 6.0 EH/s

CEO Tyler Page stated: "We are delighted to announce we achieved our initial corporate buildout goal of 6 EH/s during the month of April." The company maintained operations and capital expenditures through ongoing production without shareholder dilution.

Cipher operates primarily from its Odessa facility, which accounts for over 5 EH/s of capacity, with approximately 1 EH/s distributed across three additional sites. The company completed energization of new Bitmain and MicroBT miners during April.

The company ended April with approximately 427 Bitcoin held in treasury after selling the month's production to cover operational expenses.

Cipher Mining Announces May 2023 Operational Update

Press Release Date: June 1, 2023

Key Production Metrics for May 2023:
Bitcoin Mined: 493 BTC
Bitcoin Sold: 513 BTC
Bitcoin Held: 407 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 59,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate: 6.0 EH/s

Cipher Mining achieved record bitcoin production during May 2023, representing approximately a 21% increase compared to the previous month. The company completed the first full month of operations at its Odessa facility with over 59,000 rigs deployed.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "We are delighted to announce we achieved record production during the month of May" and emphasized that "With the first phase of our buildout at Odessa now complete, we are excited to turn our attention to completing the final phase."

The company reported that 3,120 Canaan rigs have shipped and are awaiting arrival at facilities. Management indicated they are on track to achieve 7.2 EH/s capacity by the end of Q3 2023.

Cipher completed May with potential to mine approximately 17.4 bitcoins daily while funding operating expenses and capital expenditures through ongoing operations. The company also invested capital at what management described as cyclically low equipment prices during the month.

Cipher Mining Announces June 2023 Operational Update

July 5, 2023

NEW YORK, July 05, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ:CIFR) today released its unaudited production and operations update for June 2023.

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 360
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 41
BTC Sold: 350
BTC Held: 417
Deployed Mining Rigs: 65,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate (EH/s): 6.7

(Approximate values)

In June, Cipher continued to expand operations at its Odessa facility and finished June with the potential to mine up to 17.1 bitcoin per day.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "The month of June featured challenging operating conditions. Texas experienced record high temperatures, weak wind production and record demand for power as we entered the critical 4CP summer months. Against this backdrop, we are delighted to announce production numbers that reflect not only our ability to navigate tough operating conditions and manage our mining fleet during extreme temperatures, but also our optimization of production through power sales."

The company introduced a Power Sales Equivalent BTC metric representing incremental power sales for June expressed in bitcoin terms, offering insight into monthly revenue potential beyond bitcoin production alone.

Page added: "With the extreme demand for power in June, we also saw our Odessa PPA counterparty use a significant amount of their annual curtailment budget, which demonstrates the value of bitcoin mining as an industry in supporting the grid at its most vulnerable times."

Regarding expansion efforts: "As we continue to expand our mining capacity, we aim to deliver operational excellence, even in the hot Texas summer. Our team focuses on efficient optimization of the portfolio, whether that is through the mix of rigs in use, underclocking our machines, or selling power back to the grid at the most valuable times. We are also pleased to announce that we have taken delivery of all 11,000 Canaan rigs that we recently purchased. And with the final build out of the infrastructure in the next few months, we are well on track to deliver on our goal of 7.2 EH/s by the end of Q3."

Cipher received delivery of all 11,000 Canaan rigs recently purchased and incrementally began energizing the new rigs during June, bringing total deployed rig count to approximately 65,000. Cipher produced approximately 360 BTC in June, representing a 27 percent decrease in production relative to May. As part of regular treasury management, Cipher sold approximately 350 BTC in June and executed its first bitcoin futures and options trades, ending the month with a balance of approximately 417 BTC.

About Cipher: Cipher is an emerging technology company focused on development and operation of bitcoin mining data centers. Cipher is dedicated to expanding and strengthening the Bitcoin network's critical infrastructure. The company aims to be a market leader in bitcoin mining growth and innovation.

Investor Contact: Josh Kane, josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media Contact: Ryan Dicovitsky / Kendal Till, Dukas Linden Public Relations, CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces July 2023 Operational Update

Date: August 1, 2023

Key Metrics (July 2023):
BTC Mined: 418
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 28
BTC Sold: 318
BTC Held: 518
Deployed Mining Rigs: 66,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate: 6.8 EH/s

Cipher Mining expanded operations at its Odessa facility during July, achieving approximately 16.5 bitcoins per day mining potential. The company deployed around 66,000 mining rigs total.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "We are delighted to announce that we have increased our production" despite challenging conditions, including extreme Texas temperatures and lower wind production compared to the previous year.

The company received all 11,000 Canaan rigs purchased recently and reported satisfaction with their performance. Management indicated the company remains on schedule to reach 7.2 EH/s self-mining capacity by Q3's conclusion.

Production rose roughly 16% from the previous month. Bitcoin sold during the period totaled approximately 318 units, while inventory increased to 518 units as capital expenditures declined near the completion of the Odessa expansion.

Investor Relations: josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media: CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces August 2023 Operational Update

Release Date: September 1, 2023

Key Production Metrics:
Bitcoin Mined: 357 BTC
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 45 BTC
BTC Sold: 355 BTC
BTC Held: 519 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 68,000
Operating Hash Rate: 6.8 EH/s

The company completed infrastructure expansion at its Odessa facility during August. CEO Tyler Page noted: "Our team delivered another solid month of production in August" with record Texas temperatures creating significant curtailment from their power provider.

All mining rigs were deployed onsite by month-end, with expected energization in early September. This development would increase the self-mining hash rate to 7.2 EH/s.

Cipher secured a $10 million term credit facility with Coinbase, expanding its treasury management toolkit for future use.

August production declined approximately 15% compared to July. The company sold most of its mined bitcoin as part of regular treasury management processes, maintaining approximately 519 BTC in reserves.

Cipher Mining Announces September 2023 Operational Update

Press Release Date: October 2, 2023

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR)

Key Highlights - September 2023 Metrics:
BTC Mined: 416
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 33
BTC Sold: 382
BTC Held: 553
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate: 7.2 EH/s

CEO Tyler Page announced: "We have completed the final phase of the build-out of the Odessa facility and achieved our Q3 goal of 7.2 EH/s of bitcoin mining capacity across our portfolio."

He noted the company delivered strong production despite "record temperatures and demand, as well as significant periods of curtailment from our power provider."

Cipher finalized its Odessa data center, bringing total deployed rigs to approximately 70,000. September production of roughly 416 BTC represented a 16% increase compared to August. The company sold approximately 382 BTC during the month and maintained a balance of roughly 553 BTC.

Investor Relations: josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media Contact: CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces October 2023 Operational Update

Release Date: November 1, 2023

Key Production Metrics for October 2023:
Bitcoin mined: 428 BTC
Power sales equivalent BTC: 16
Bitcoin sold: 466
Bitcoin held: 516
Deployed mining rigs: 70,000
Month-end operating hash rate: 7.2 EH/s

Cipher increased production by approximately 3% compared to September, despite the network's hash rate growing 9% year-over-year. The company maintained robust operations with its Odessa facility fully operational.

CEO Tyler Page remarked that the firm achieved "another solid month of production with Odessa fully up and running" and noted the capability to produce up to 14.9 BTC daily.

Management scheduled an earnings call for November 8th at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss Q3 2023 results and growth initiatives.

The company sold approximately 466 BTC during October as part of standard treasury management procedures.

Investor Relations: josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media: CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces November 2023 Operational Update

Publication Date: December 4, 2023

Key Operational Metrics for November 2023:
Bitcoin Mined: 433
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 9
Bitcoin Sold: 391
Bitcoin Held: 558
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate: 7.2 EH/s

Tyler Page, CEO of Cipher Mining, noted that "Cipher's production really hit its stride in November, when we mined more bitcoin than October despite having a day less on the calendar." He highlighted that the Odessa facility achieved an all-time high site hash rate of approximately 6.24 EH/s and that the company finished November with the potential to mine up to approximately 15.7 bitcoin per day.

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR) operates bitcoin mining data centers focused on developing critical infrastructure for the Bitcoin network.

Investor Relations: Josh Kane, josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media: Dukas Linden Public Relations, CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces December 2023 Operational Update

Press Release Date: January 2, 2024

Key Production Metrics:
Bitcoin Mined: 465 BTC
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 16 BTC
BTC Sold: 228 BTC
BTC Held: 796 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Operating Hash Rate: 7.2 EH/s

The company achieved approximately 7.4% production growth in December despite network hash rate increases of 6.3%. CEO Tyler Page stated: "Cipher delivered another month of increased production in December, despite continued growth in network hash rate."

He highlighted contributing factors including elevated Bitcoin transaction fees, favorable Texas mining conditions, and operational team performance.

Cipher concluded 2023 with approximately $120 million in total liquidity (Bitcoin and cash combined) and zero debt. The company announced plans to acquire additional mining equipment for expansion initiatives.

Investor Relations: Josh Kane, josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media: Dukas Linden Public Relations, CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces January 2024 Operational Update

Publication Date: February 1, 2024

Key Production Metrics for January 2024:
Bitcoin Mined: 371 BTC
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 8
BTC Sold: 34
BTC Held: 1,132
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate: 7.2 EH/s

Tyler Page, Chief Executive Officer, noted that January presented "challenging market conditions for bitcoin miners" with increased network hash rates and reduced transaction fees. The company experienced operational curtailment due to elevated Texas power prices during cold weather. Despite these headwinds, the organization maintained positive cash generation and expanded its bitcoin reserves while receiving initial shipments of Bitmain S21 mining equipment.

Production declined approximately 20.4% compared to December as overall network hash rate increased roughly 3.5%. The company executed treasury management activities, selling 34 BTC while accumulating holdings to approximately 1,132 BTC by month's end.

Cipher Mining develops and operates bitcoin mining data centers, focusing on strengthening Bitcoin network infrastructure through technological advancement and strategic partnerships.

Next Earnings Call: March 5th

Cipher Mining Announces February 2024 Operational Update

Publication Date: March 1, 2024

Key Operational Metrics:
Bitcoin Mined: 334 BTC
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 2 BTC
BTC Sold: 33 BTC
BTC Holdings (Month-End): 1,433 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Operating Hash Rate: 7.4 EH/s

The company produced approximately 334 bitcoins during February while the network's average monthly hash rate increased roughly 9.4 percent. Through regular treasury management, Cipher sold approximately 33 bitcoins that month, concluding with holdings near 1,433 bitcoins.

Cipher scheduled its Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 results conference call for Tuesday, March 5th at 8:00 AM Eastern Time. The webcast and replay were made available through the investor relations website at investors.ciphermining.com.

Cipher focuses on developing and operating bitcoin mining data centers while working to strengthen Bitcoin network infrastructure through technological advancement and strategic partnerships.

Cipher Mining Announces March 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: April 1, 2024

Key Performance Metrics:
Bitcoin Mined: 316 BTC
Power Sales Equivalent BTC: 9 BTC
BTC Sold: 8 BTC
BTC Holdings: 1,741 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Operating Hash Rate: 7.6 EH/s

CEO Tyler Page stated: "Cipher delivered another solid performance in March, which marks the completion of a remarkably strong first quarter of production." He noted the company maintained uptime despite power provider curtailments at the Odessa facility and growing network hash rates.

Page highlighted bitcoin inventory growth to approximately 1,741 BTC and construction progress on Q2 2024 expansions at Bear and Chief facilities, plus early work at the Black Pearl site scheduled for 2025 launch.

During March, Cipher produced approximately 316 BTC while the network hash rate increased roughly 4.7%. The company sold 8 BTC as part of regular treasury management activities.

Management emphasized strong positioning heading into the April bitcoin halving, citing expanding hash rate capacity, competitive unit economics, and timely expansion execution.

Cipher Mining Announces April 2024 Operational Update

May 1, 2024

NEW YORK, May 01, 2024 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ:CIFR) today released its unaudited production and operations update for April 2024.

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 296
BTC Sold: 0
BTC Held: 2,033
Deployed Mining Rigs: 70,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate (EH/s): 7.7

Note: Includes April power sales estimates (based on current meter data and nodal prices) equivalent to 3 bitcoin (using month-end bitcoin price of $60,171)

In April, block 840,000 ushered in Bitcoin's fifth epoch and reduced the issuance of new bitcoin to 3.125 roughly every 10 minutes going forward. After a brief spike in network transaction fees that produced daily revenues well in excess of a typical day prior to the halving, production normalized for the final few days of the month. Against this backdrop, Cipher continued to build its inventory while making good progress on site expansions.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "With the halving behind us, we have finally arrived at the moment Cipher has been anticipating for years, and going forward we look to reap the benefits of our positioning in the market as a low-cost producer. We look forward to updating investors on our operations, financials and growth plans next week on our Q1 2024 earnings call."

The company will host an earnings call on Tuesday, May 7th at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

Cipher produced approximately 296 BTC in April amidst average monthly network hash rate increasing approximately 4.8%. As part of its regular treasury management process, Cipher sold 0 BTC in April, ending the month with a balance of approximately 2,033 BTC.

About Cipher: Cipher is an emerging technology company focused on the development and operation of bitcoin mining data centers. The company is dedicated to expanding and strengthening the Bitcoin network's critical infrastructure.

Investor Contact: Josh Kane, Head of Investor Relations, josh.kane@ciphermining.com
Media Contact: Ryan Dicovitsky / Kendal Till, Dukas Linden Public Relations, CipherMining@DLPR.com

Cipher Mining Announces May 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: June 4, 2024

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 166 (includes ~8 BTC equivalent from power sales)
BTC Sold: 50
BTC Held: 2,142
Deployed Mining Rigs: 71,000
Operating Hash Rate: 8.0 EH/s

Cipher maintained positive cash flows during May following the network halving while expanding operations at Bear and Chief facilities. The company's Odessa data center power provider had already used substantial annual curtailment capacity, positioning Cipher favorably for potential summer price opportunities given its low-cost contracted power.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "With our power hedged at a low price, strong balance sheet, and continuing expansion plans, we expect to thrive in this post-halving environment."

The company announced a signed term sheet for a Cotulla, Texas data center site priced at $2.5 million, offering at least 70 MW capacity in 2026 with potential expansion to 200 MW. Closing was anticipated in early July pending due diligence completion.

The company produced approximately 166 BTC in May and sold roughly 50 BTC as part of regular treasury management operations.

Cipher Mining Announces June 2024 Operational Update

Jul 2, 2024

NEW YORK, July 02, 2024 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ:CIFR) today released its unaudited production and operations update for June 2024.

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 176
BTC Sold: 103
BTC Held: 2,209
Deployed Mining Rigs: 75,000
Month End Operating Hash Rate (EH/s): 8.6

Note: Includes June power sales estimates (based on current meter data and nodal prices) equivalent to 6 bitcoin (using month-end bitcoin price of $63,370)

"In June, Cipher delivered another solid month of production and continued to expand operations, with energization of the 30 MW expansions at each of its Bear and Chief data centers."

CEO Tyler Page stated: "At Bear we have now received and installed all the new mining rigs, and we expect to finish installing the remainder of the new mining rigs at Chief later this month."

Cipher produced approximately 176 BTC in June. Through regular treasury management, Cipher sold approximately 103 BTC in June, ending the month with a balance of approximately 2,209 BTC.

Cipher Mining Announces August 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: September 3, 2024

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR)

Key Production Metrics:
BTC Mined: ~160 BTC
BTC Sold: 136 BTC
BTC Held: 2,284 BTC
Deployed Mining Rigs: 76,000
Month-end Hash Rate: 9.1 EH/s

Note: Includes power sales estimates equivalent to 9 BTC (calculated at month-end bitcoin price of $58,516)

The company produced approximately 160 BTC in August 2024. The statement notes August presented challenges typical of summer operations in Texas, including "meaningful curtailment from its power provider at the Odessa data center during the month."

CEO Tyler Page commented: "The Cipher team has been very active this summer managing operational and electricity pricing risks in the Texas heat while continuing the on-schedule construction at Black Pearl."

Cipher qualified as a well-known seasoned issuer and filed a new shelf registration statement. The company announced a 300 MW site acquisition expected to close that month, bringing total power portfolio capacity to over 2.5 GW.

Page noted the company views "liquidating portions of our bitcoin holdings over time as a potential source for funding accretive growth."

Cipher Mining Announces September 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: October 3, 2024

Key Production Metrics:
Bitcoin mined: ~155 BTC
Bitcoin sold: ~923 BTC
Bitcoin held: ~1,512 BTC
Deployed mining rigs: 78,000
Operating hashrate: 9.3 EH/s

The company successfully navigated the final month of 4CP pricing in Texas while completing preparations for a mining fleet upgrade at Odessa. Construction continues at the Black Pearl data center, targeting energization by end of Q2 2025.

CEO Tyler Page stated: "With the anticipated completion of the Odessa upgrade by year-end, we will have one of the most efficient" mining operations available.

A significant corporate development involved acquiring the 300 MW Barber Lake site, largely funded through bitcoin treasury sales. Page noted the company exchanges cryptocurrency holdings "opportunistically, with the goal of maximizing shareholder returns" and views large-scale energized sites as rare HPC hosting candidates.

The company develops and operates data centers for HPC hosting and bitcoin mining, focusing on infrastructure expansion and serving major computing companies worldwide.

Cipher Mining Announces October 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: November 4, 2024

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 168
BTC Sold: 248
BTC Held: 1,428
Deployed Mining Rigs: 77,000
Operating Hash Rate: 10.7 EH/s

Note: October figures include power sales estimates totaling 4.5 BTC equivalent (calculated at the month-end price of $69,278 per bitcoin) plus 30 BTC from joint venture data centers representing Cipher's ownership stake.

Cipher produced approximately 168 BTC during October 2024. The company sold roughly 248 BTC as part of its treasury management strategy, concluding the month with a balance of approximately 1,428 BTC.

The organization's teams "began executing on the upgrade of the mining fleet at Odessa and continued to develop the new Black Pearl data center" during October.

Management referenced a third-quarter business update call conducted on October 31st for additional company context.

Cipher Mining Announces November 2024 Operational Update

Press Release Date: December 3, 2024

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 202
BTC Sold: 234
BTC Held: 1,383
Deployed Mining Rigs: 76,000
Operating Hash Rate (EH/s): 12.0
Fleet Efficiency (J/TH): 20.8

CEO Tyler Page stated the company expects to finish upgrading its Odessa mining fleet by year's end, aiming to establish "one of the most efficient fleets of mining rigs in the industry." He added that Black Pearl data center development remains scheduled for energization by end of Q2 2025, with substation work now underway.

Cipher closed its acquisition of the 100 MW Stingray site in November, expected to energize during 2026. The company's combined portfolio and development pipeline now encompasses more than 2.6 gigawatts across 11 sites.

The company produced approximately 202 BTC in November and sold approximately 234 BTC as part of regular treasury management, ending the month with a balance near 1,383 BTC.

Cipher operates industrial-scale data centers focusing on bitcoin mining and HPC hosting, aiming for leadership in innovation and serving major HPC companies globally.

Cipher Mining Announces December 2024 Operational Update

Release Date: January 6, 2025

Key Highlights:
Bitcoin Mined: 234 BTC
Bitcoin Sold: 271 BTC
Bitcoin Holdings: 1,344 BTC (includes ~345 BTC pledged as collateral)
Deployed Mining Rigs: 75,000
Operating Hashrate: 13.5 EH/s
Fleet Efficiency: 18.9 J/TH

CEO Tyler Page stated: "We are thrilled to announce the completion of the Odessa upgrade, giving Cipher ~13.5 EH/s of self-mining hashrate with one of the most efficient fleets."

Co-President Patrick Kelly noted the company deployed enhanced technology to continue operations during power curtailments, hashing for roughly 80 hours when they would have otherwise faced disruptions despite the provider exercising approximately 20% of annual curtailment hours.

The company completed its mining fleet upgrade at Odessa and made substantial progress on the Black Pearl data center development. December production included power sales estimates equivalent to 2 BTC and 31 BTC from joint venture data centers representing Cipher's ownership stake.

Cipher Mining Announces January 2025 Operational Update

Press Release - February 3, 2025

Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ: CIFR)

Production Highlights:

The company continues advancing its Black Pearl site, approaching completion of Phase 1, which spans over 100,000 square feet. Management is simultaneously engaging with prospective tenants and financing partners to develop Cipher's position as an HPC data center leader.

Cipher Mining Announces February 2025 Operational Update

Mar 4, 2025

NEW YORK, March 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ:CIFR) today released its unaudited production and operations update for February 2025.

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 180
BTC Sold: 235
BTC Held: 1,032
Deployed Mining Rigs: 75,000
Month End Operating Hashrate (EH/s): 13.5
Month End Fleet Efficiency (J/TH): 18.9

Note 1: Includes February power sales estimates (based on current meter data and nodal prices) equivalent to 4 bitcoin (using month-end bitcoin price of $86,154) and 24 BTC mined at JV data centers representing Cipher's ownership.
Note 2: Includes ~394 BTC pledged as collateral.

"Cipher delivered strong production in February despite a brief, planned shutdown at our Odessa site for annual high-voltage electrical maintenance." The work was completed successfully by the Operations team. Cipher reported earnings on February 25th, with a webcast replay available at https://investors.ciphermining.com.

Cipher produced approximately 180 BTC in February. Through regular treasury management, the company sold approximately 235 BTC during the month, ending with a balance of roughly 1,032 BTC.

The press release includes a photo showing "Recent arrival of the first substation transformer at our Black Pearl site."

About Cipher: Cipher focuses on developing and operating industrial-scale data centers for bitcoin mining and HPC hosting, aiming to lead in innovation, mining growth, and data center construction.

Cipher Mining Announces March 2025 Operational Update

Date: April 4, 2025

Key Production Metrics:
Bitcoin mined: 210 BTC
Bitcoin sold: 206 BTC
Bitcoin held: 1,034 BTC
Deployed mining rigs: 75,000
Operating hashrate: 13.5 EH/s
Fleet efficiency: 18.9 J/TH

The company maintained consistent production throughout March. Management noted that "the company has continued to make substantial progress on the construction of Black Pearl, highlighted by the ahead of schedule receipt of our second transformer for the site."

The organization emphasized its confidence in the HPC (high-performance computing) sector, stating it "maintains high conviction in the HPC opportunity, and in the significant value of our pipeline for HPC compute."

As part of regular operations, Cipher sold approximately 206 BTC during the month while maintaining a balance of roughly 1,034 BTC (with approximately 394 BTC pledged as collateral).

Cipher Mining Inc. operates industrial-scale data centers for bitcoin mining and HPC hosting services. The company aims to serve as a market leader in data center innovation and development.

Cipher Mining Surpasses Hashrate Growth Forecasts at Black Pearl and Announces June 2025 Operational Update

Jul 2, 2025

NEW YORK, July 02, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cipher Mining Inc. (NASDAQ:CIFR) today announced that Phase I of Black Pearl is now delivering ~3.4 EH/s of self-mining capacity, exceeding the Company's prior guidance of 2.5 EH/s for the second quarter of 2025. Hashrate will continue to increase at the site through the third quarter of 2025 as new mining rigs continue to be delivered in scheduled batches, gradually replacing legacy units. Cipher's total self-mining hashrate has now reached ~16.8 EH/s, and the Company maintains its expectations to scale to ~23.1 EH/s upon completion of the installation.

In addition, the Company today released its unaudited production and operations update for June 2025. While the report includes initial contributions from Black Pearl, which started hashing at the end of the month, the production numbers also reflect the Company's strategic decision to curtail more deliberately as part of its proactive 4CP avoidance strategy. This approach allowed the Company to avoid costly 4CP penalties and maintain its position as having some of the lowest power costs in the industry. Insights from June will inform further refinements to the curtailment model for the remainder of the summer.

Key Metrics:
BTC Mined: 160
BTC Sold: 58
BTC Held: 1,063
Deployed Mining Rigs: 104,000
Month End Operating Hashrate (EH/s): 16.8
Month End Fleet Efficiency (J/TH): 20.8

Note: Includes June power sales estimates (based on current meter data and nodal prices) equivalent to ~5 bitcoin (using month-end bitcoin price of $107,221) and ~19 BTC mined at JV data centers representing Cipher's ownership.

Cipher produced ~160 BTC in June. As part of its regular treasury management process, Cipher sold ~58 BTC in June, ending the month with a balance of ~1,063 BTC.

About Cipher: Cipher is focused on the development and operation of industrial-scale data centers for bitcoin mining and HPC hosting. Cipher aims to be a market leader in innovation, including in bitcoin mining growth, data center construction and as a hosting partner to the world's largest HPC companies. https://www.ciphermining.com/
